items.title: Apple’s MacBook Neo is winning over a new generation of buyers
items.content: The MacBook Neo shipped 1.1 million units in its first weeks on sale, IDC estimates, as Apple pushes deeper into the mainstream laptop market.

items.title: Sneak peek at new Siri app reveals Apple’s plans to take on ChatGPT and more
items.content: New renders offer a closer look at Apple’s planned AI overhaul for iOS 27, including a redesigned Siri experience and standalone Siri app.

items.title: These special phone and app features can help protect you from spyware
items.content: Apple, Meta, and Google offer special security modes that provide your devices more secure against targeted spyware attacks. Here are how those modes work, what they do, and h

items.title: Apple says Epic lawsuit shouldn’t reshape App Store rules for all developers
items.content: Apple is asking the Supreme Court to narrow the App Store injunction won by Epic Games and overturn the court’s contempt ruling over external payment fees.

items.title: New Microsoft tool lets devs spin up AI behavior tests using text descriptions
items.content: Microsoft on Tuesday took the wraps off Adaptive Spec-driven Scoring for Evaluation and Regression Testing, an open source framework for spinning up AI evaluations.

items.title: Microsoft launches Scout, an OpenClaw-inspired personal assistant
items.content: Launched at Build, Microsoft Scout is a new AI assistant meant to bring the power and flexibility of OpenClaw into the Microsoft 365 system.

items.title: Microsoft offers devs a better way to control AI agent behavior
items.content: The specification lets developer, compliance, and security teams define their own policies for agents to follow in portable policy files.

items.title: Nvidia chases $200B CPU market with AI agent PCs from Microsoft, Dell, and HP
items.content: If Nvidia has cracked a way to bring AI agents easily, safely, and usefully to the masses, it could — and should — be big.

items.title: Trump Administration Turns to a New Rationale to Justify Old Tariffs
items.content: The administration has settled on a more legally and politically durable way to impose tariffs, but some say the focus on forced labor is merely a pretext for protectionism.

items.title: Trump Administration Fights Court Order to Refund Some Tariffs
items.content: The administration has started to repay some of the money, but has signaled it may make it harder for certain businesses to claim the full amount they are owed.

items.title: Bari Weiss Speaks on Scott Pelley’s ’60 Minutes’ Firing: ‘That’s the Path That He Chose’
items.content: In her first public comments on the firing, Ms. Weiss, the CBS News editor in chief, said that the longtime correspondent had “broken” the trust in the newsroom.

items.title: European Union Outlines Plan to Reduce Dependence on American Tech
items.content: The 27-nation European Union outlined how it hopes to expand the region’s data centers, semiconductors and cloud computing capabilities.
